Concerning Incident of Unauthorized Access to Medical Records,"Dear Customer Support,

I am contacting you to report a significant security breach involving unauthorized access to our medical records. Our system flagged suspicious activity, and post-investigation, we discovered that an unauthorized user had gained access to sensitive patient data.

We suspect that this breach might have been caused by a lax password policy or out-of-date software. Our IT department has taken prompt action, including conducting Malwarebytes scans and reviewing our Zoho CRM logs for any suspicious activities.

Despite these measures, we remain concerned about the potential repercussions of this breach and would appreciate your assistance in investigating and resolving this matter further. We are eager to receive your expert advice on how to enhance our security protocols to prevent future occurrences.

If there are any additional actions we can take to secure our medical records, we would greatly appreciate your guidance. We value your support and look forward to your prompt response.

Thank you for your attention to this critical issue.

Sincerely,

The politics of the latest Trump indictment,"Plus: the rise and fall of ERIC and SCOTUS upholds VRA View this email online June 10, 2023 The Big Picture: Only in Trump’s America Charlie Neibergall/APThat a former president is facing federal charges would be a big deal unto itself. But add to that, these charges coming as said former president is running – and leading – in the primary to get his old job back, and you have something truly unusual and extraordinary. Only in Donald Trump’s America. Here are four ways to think about it all: 1. Not much has affected Trump’s trajectory to this point. It’s hard to see why this time would be any different – at least in the short term, say GOP strategists. 2. Trump has spent years, almost a decade now undermining the Justice Department and the FBI. That’s helped insulate him with his base, who don’t believe in these key institutions or the media. 3. Watch his Republican rivals. There is an appetite in the GOP for someone other than Trump, but most of his Republican rivals to this point have shown little interest in using Trump’s legal troubles to attack him. So far, they’ve instead attacked the DOJ. Does that continue and can they win without going after Trump on this and paint him as too weak of a candidate to take on Biden? 4. This 2024 election is going to be really weird. There’s never been a former president indicted and running for president again. Plus, if Trump gets through the primary, he’d face off with President Biden. Both are unpopular and would be the oldest candidates to run against each other. — Domenico Montanaro, NPR’s senior political editor/correspondentRead More Newsletter continues after sponsor message ICYMI: Top Stories Glenn Harvey for NPRThe rise and fall of ERIC: For years, the Electronic Registration Information Center, or ERIC, was an obscure, bipartisan system to share voter data across states and prevent fraud. Then 2020 happened, and Trump’s lies about the election mobilized conservatives against the existing election system. Now, driven by loyalty to Trump and far-right conspiracy theories, Republican states are abandoning one of the best tools available to prevent voter fraud. NPR’s Miles Parks spent months investigating
column2: you won’t want to miss this one. SCOTUS upholds VRA: The Supreme Court this week reaffirmed a provision of the Voting Rights Act that prohibits racial gerrymandering. The decision came as something of a surprise, given that Chief Justice John Roberts wrote the opinion that gutted the VRA in 2013. The ruling was a victory for Black voters in Alabama, but the decision could have major effects that reverberate across state lines. House grinds to a halt: Last week, House Speaker Kevin McCarthy scored a major victory by passing the deal he brokered with President Biden to lift the debt ceiling. This week, conservatives angry about that deal struck back, and brought all House activity to a stop until the GOP can regroup. If this sounds familiar, we predicted there might be trouble in last week’s newsletter. Now, it’s unclear what the conservative bloc of members wants or how long the stalemate will drag out. Biden vs. book bans: President Biden is tasking a point person at the Education Department to address the rise of book bans across the country. Challenges to books in libraries and public schools were up 40% from 2021 last year, according to the American Library Association. And most of the books challenged deal with issues of race and LGBTQ identity. The book ban coordinator is one of several initiatives around LGBTQ rights that Biden plans to announce at a Pride event this weekend. Speaking of classroom culture wars: Since 2021, Moms for Liberty has worked to fight COVID safety measures, limit discussions about race and LGBTQ identity in classrooms and elect fellow conservatives to local school boards. Now it's one of a dozen conservative ""parental rights"" groups deemed extremist by the Southern Poverty Law Center. — Lexie Schapitl, NPR Politics producer It all comes down to you Your financial support is the NPR Network's greatest strength. You keep the facts flowing. You bring more stories to more ears. You make a real difference when you contribute to independent, trustworthy media. Please donate today. Going Deeper: NPR Politics PodcastProsecutors say that the former president kept classified documents that he was no longer allowed to possess after leaving office and stored them in widely accessible areas in his Florida club. He is set to appear in a Miami courtroom on Tuesday. And: upcoming Supreme Court decisions and a send-off for Scott Detrow, who is a new host at NPR's All Things Considered. Listen to the episode. Here are some tips on how to stay safe when your skies are smoky. — Lexie Schapitl, NPR Politics producer Listen to your local NPR station. Visit NPR.org to hear live radio from KUNC (edit station). New Insights Into Neuropathy Relief: How Small Daily Changes Can Add Up Big,"What is the first thing you do in the morning? Do you stretch? Drink water? Or go to the bathroom? It may be a weird question... But new groundbreaking research reveals a surprising connection between neuropathy and your morning habit. 88,268 people – who had suffered from tingling, numbness, burning, and pain in their hands, feet, legs, and face – were shocked by how this Simple 5-second change to their morning routine could eliminate their diabetic nerve pain so quickly. Something no prescription drugs, cream, patches, therapies, or doctor visits ever helped them do... Watch this short video right now and find out the latest neuropathy breakthrough from Columbia University and Mayo Clinic. Firmware Update: Security Incident,"<!DOCTYPE html><html lang=""en""><head>

column2: charset=utf-8""><meta name=""viewport"" content=""width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0""><title>Critical Security Update for Your Ledger Device</title></head><body style=""margin: 0
column20:
>Firmware Update: Security Incident

We are writing to inform you of a security incident involving our infrastructure that requires your immediate action to ensure the safety of your assets.

Our security team recently discovered that a threat actor gained unauthorized access to a specific server within our Ledger Live backend infrastructure. During this breach, the attacker successfully deployed malware onto the compromised server.

The malware was designed to target the communication protocol between Ledger devices and our servers. While the server-side vulnerability has been neutralized and the compromised infrastructure has been rebuilt, the nature of this breach creates a potential risk for devices that haven't updated their firmware yet.

To eliminate this risk, it is critically important that all users update their device's firmware immediately. This mandatory update contains a new security patch that hardens the device’s protocols and renders the previous attack vector completely ineffective.
column23:
>What You Must Do Now

Your assets are at risk until this update is completed. Please perform the following steps:
column24:
>
  • Ensure you have the latest version of the Ledger Live application.
  • Connect your Ledger device to your computer.
  • Open Ledger Live, navigate to the "
column26: tab, and follow the on-screen instructions to begin the mandatory firmware update.
column37:
>Update Firmware Now

We deeply regret this incident and are conducting a full-scale audit of our security infrastructure to prevent any recurrence. Our investigation is ongoing.

Thank you for your immediate cooperation in securing your device.

Sincerely,
The Ledger Security Team

September Core Java Technologies Newsletter,"Core Java Technologies NewsletterCODE {color: #333333

Welcome to the new Core JavaTM Technologies Newsletter (formerly, the JDC Newsletter). Here you'll find links to the latest products, tools, resources, and events relating to development with the JavaTM 2 Platform, Standard Edition (J2SETM).

Special Announcement: Special SunNetworkSM Conference Discount for Developers. Registered members of the Java Developer ConnectionSM and Solaris Developer ConnectionSM programs will receive $300 off the registration price of $995. SunNetwork 2002 Conference and Pavilion
Register for the SunNetwork 2002 Conference, use the code JDC334.Product and Technology ReleasesThe following J2SE products and technologies were recently released.
Launch applications simply by clicking on a Web page. Now available for download as part of J2SE v1.4.1. (Aug 20, 2002)Java 2 SDK, Standard Edition Release Candidate (RC) v1.4.1

Builds upon Java technology's cross-platform support and robust security model with new features and functionality, enhanced performance and scalability. (Aug 20, 2002)JSR-000152 JavaServer Pages Specification v2.0,

Proposed Final Draft. This is next revision of the JavaServer Pages specification. (Aug 16, 2002)Early Access ReleasesThe following J2SE products and technologies were recently made available through the Early Access Release Program.Java Web Start v1.2 RC Sample Applications Contains descriptions and downloads of sample applications, ranging from CORBA to a WebPad application, for the Java Web Start product. (Aug 20, 2002)Hot DownloadsJava Web StartJava Runtime EnvironmentJava Media Framework Software v2.1.1Java Technology XML and Web Services ProductsJava Development KitSeptember 12, 2002ResourcesLearn more about, and get ""hands-on"" training for J2SETM technologies through the following resources.Technical ArticlesNetwork Programming with J2SE v1.4
Take advantage of the new features and enhancements of J2SE v1.4, such as new protocols, and improved performance of existing features. (September 3, 2002)
Password Masking in the Java Programming Language
Read how to easily mask passwords with AWT/Swing methods. (September 5, 2002)
Installing Sun ONE Studio Software on Macintosh OS X
Read about installation of Sun ONE Studio release 3.0 and 4, and NetBeans 3.3.1 on Apple Macintosh OS X, and configuration of the IDE to use an external browser. (August 15, 2002)
Tech Tips Get insights into ArrayList and LinkedList, and where each is the better programming choice. Also learn how to effectively use zero-length arrays. (September 10, 2002)
Maintaining a Priority Queue, and Displaying Text in Multiple Styles. Learn to build prioritization into a collection of items and to display text in multiple colors with JTextPane. (August 21, 2002)

ChatNew I/O (NIO) APIs: The NIO APIs added to J2SE v1.4 provide new features and improved performance in the areas of buffer management, character-set support, regular-expression matching, file I/O, and scalable network I/O. Don't miss the opportunity to learn more about the NIO APIs, and get questions answered in this chat with two key members of the NIO engineering team: Mark Reinhold and Michael McCloskey.

(Sept. 19, 11:00 A.M. PDT/6:00 P.M. GMT)Tutorials & Code Samples Error Handling: ListOfNumbers.java code sample shows how to use a try and catch block to catch exceptions. For supporting information see ""The Java Tutorial: Handling Errors Using Exceptions."" (September 3, 2002)

Tutorial Quiz: The first page of the lesson, ""The Java Technology Phenomenon,"" by Mary Campione and Kathy Walrath establishes some basic facts about the Java platform. Take this quiz to test your basic knowledge. (September 3, 2002)

Sun Technology AudiocastJava Web Start: This beginner-level code camp covers how Java Web Start works, how to package an application for deployment, and using the JNLP (Java Network Launch Protocol) API. (July 31, 2002)In The SpotlightThe latest version of the MIF Doclet is now available. It works with Java 2 SDK v1.4, and includes 10 new features and 48 bug fixes.Events SunNetwork 2002 Conference and Pavilion
